item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was the greatest tale I have ever read. Nothing else, nothing more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one to be honest, it makes me want to see the factory expanded on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you cant figure out how to destroy one super toy company
Ask the other one
I believe the factory might have a little trouble competing against Dr. Wondertainment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno why, but I don't like the idea of Foundation Employees being sacrificed. Obviously gonna upvote though, this is a great read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great, now my personal headcannon involves factory being the corruption of realities that consume universe after universe.
The one the administrator was fleeing from in hopes of stopping it one day. I think mackenzie proposal…

Well. I genuinely wonder what would it take to at least repel the factory from our universe.
I am thinking an altered 3125 would not be enough…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every time someone mentions the Fairies I keep thinking of an entire race of Cirno’s

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something something that's just like your headcanon man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Firstly, if you're going to make a forum post that isn't in response to a conversation, just click "New Post", don't respond to other people. Secondly, there is no "real SCP-001". The 001 slot is designed in a way that no proposal is the "real" one. They could be all real, some real, none real, but there's no definitive answer. If you have a personal headcanon as to what SCP-001 is, that's fine, but it's not the definitive "real SCP-001".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you find the real SCP-001 which I have done

item.title: Re: A question to the creator (or anyone who has an answer)
item.content:encoded: Nope, never read that.

item.title: A question to the creator (or anyone who has an answer)
item.content:encoded: I kind of had the feeling that The Factory was kind of based off of the working conditions around the industrial revolution/WW1. Like, the writer got the idea from "The Jungle" by Upton Sinclair".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has and always probably will be my favorite 001. I love that the Foundation has a price to pay for even existing. And I also love that there's a reason for SCPs suddenly appearing out of nowhere in huge numbers. Quite a few SCPs mention their ancient origin, but the cold hard truth is that if all of these XK class Keters have been around for eons, the world would have ended long ago…

…and in fact, with so many around there seems to be about a 20% chance of universal destruction per day without intervention from the foundation. Unless, of course, the Foundation is getting some heavyweight supernatural aid itself. Only Scantron's middle-school UIU mutant-foundation 001 touches on the same thing, but your story itself is much better.

One of the best entries on the whole gosh darn site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HOLY SHIT IT ALL ADDS UP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, the Fae?
As in, the same Fae that were defeated by the Sasquatches during tribal times? The same Fae who first came up with the technology that the Sasquatches used (as evidenced by the corgi subway)? The very same Fae that were, some of them at least, forced into a magical pocket dimension which they now control with their own, word-based physics?

item.title: Union Aerospace Corporation SCP style.
item.content:encoded:
So Hunger Games districts but a hell-alot worse. And Anderson reminds me of Samuel Hayden.

[Edit]
Reading the comments makes me sad because I honestly loved this post. I only have one small critique: When he mentions "seeing red" it would be neat to show his unnatural abilities by slamming his fist down on a metal table denting it in anger. A pause to me does not seem like anger to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exactly my position. The writing is too evocative for me to downvote, but at the same time I kind of hate the narrative structure (or lack thereof).

Not only does it feel rambling and nonsensical, But the story pulls fairies out its ass near the end which comes out of nowhere and doesn't really gel with the rest. I think the implication is that the unreliable narrator is lying about some or all of this, but this explanation doesn't really satisfy me on that front.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure I like this take on the Factory, or the Foundation, or the characters, but the writing here is solid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really like the factory, or this origin story for the Foundation, but it's well written and does a good job at establishing a foundation (har har) to build on, which makes a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my favorite tale, and also my favorite 001 proposal, so this is the most deserving of my +1

item.title: Re: not_a_seagull's SCP Oddessy: Bright's Proposal
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate!

item.title: not_a_seagull's SCP Oddessy: Bright's Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Item #: SCP-001 (Bright's Proposal)

Author: TheDuckman

Review:

+ Review
- Review

What I said when I finished reading this was: what the fresh fuck.

Like the other 001's this is an explainer. By that, I mean that it explains the origins of stuff. It not only explains the Foundation, but also MC&D, the GOC, the CotBG, and the UIU (I think?).

Of course, it's not just an explainer. It's a very, very, very good (and creepy) tale. It takes what you knew from the vague references of the Factory, and twists them into… whatever this is. There could also be implications about the Scarlet King in here.

Of course I upvoted. It's a good tale, a good format screw of the 001 proposal, and a great 001. Words fail me here.

Verdict: Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, because this 001 proposal is written in the form of a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that this is not a cataloged SCP. But, shouldn't it have an object class? I mean tagged with an object class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I think that was the first SPC-001 proposal I've read, and also the first article I've read that explained the Foundation's origins. After reading a lot of other ones, it's not exactly my favorite one, but I still love it. I think it tells a really interesting and compelling story, that made me always curious for what was about to come, and that explained a lot of the Foundation universe, and it ended on a excellent and sinister note.
Yay. I love the idea of an SCP-001 proposal actually being a tale, so I had fun reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i kinda feel like all the proposals are real
to some extent
that this is the dump for the real fucked up shit. the ones they need to be hidden
sure, a few are complete fiction, but thats just more misdirection

item.title: Well done.
item.content:encoded:
This line, for me, summarizes the entire concept of SCP and I will remember it each time I read any of the articles:

"We found tools, both wondrous and horrible. And we were faced with a choice."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a really well written out story, I liked that. You added a damn lot of detail to it. But really my only problem was outright claiming that the other proposals are 100% false and that this is the real one. Also, it really just put out the Foundation's origins too neatly and spreaded, I would think that they would try and hide a couple sections…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hamfisted, excessive grimdark for grimdark's sake. I was mildly interested right up until the "breeding pits" part, at which point I rolled my eyes, skimmed the rest to see precisely what I expected to see, and downvoted (not that it significantly hurts this one's standing.)

Honestly, Bright's produced some reasonably decent pieces here and there, I think, but when he actively tries to be disturbing, it's with all the finesse of a dumptruck full of 90s McFarlane action figures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to my knowledge, unless someone took the idea and ran with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Say, is the Anderson in this version of The Factory's origins related to the one that keeps popping up in several scips and tales (you know, the "Anderson" tag)?

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded: And there you have it, folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, that's bang on, sport!

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded: Nope. Not at all.

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded:
i remember one of the SCP article places the user's name in the article. Can't remember the number, but it's an ammo box and people will keep open and close it and start to countdown.

That'd be SCP-902, The Final Countdown.

Think it will be a good idea to put it in?

I'm not the author, but I'm going to say no. It'd detract from the seriousness from the piece because 1. as sirpudding stated, this is a conversation between two characters whose identities are pretty firmly set and 2. there's nothing in the piece that would necessitate inputting the reader's name (and I'm pretty sure the majority of members on the site don't have "serious"-sounding actual Foundation names like Doctor/Agent/Researcher/etc [name] because we're not a roleplay site)

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded: I meant Adam, but that was bad memory anyway.

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded: This is an O5 talking with Mann.

item.title: Re: To the author
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this Jack Adam Bright O5-1 talking with Everett Mann? Where would a ListUsers even go?

Also, while I doubt Duckman especially wants to add one, I personally think that ListUsers is a gimmick with limited usefullness, since I have trouble imaging that "sirpudding" is anyone other than me.

item.title: To the author
item.content:encoded:
i remember one of the SCP article places the user's name in the article. Can't remember the number, but it's an ammo box and people will keep open and close it and start to countdown. Think it will be a good idea to put it in?

And also, fantastic work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a fan. I liked the nod to the other GOIs but overall there was little here that interested me. The 110-Montauk reference didn't work in my opinion- (people always reference that, and it rarely works)

It's alright I guess, but underwhelming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK, so the Chaplain obviously went on to make the Church of the Broken God, Marshall and his followers went on to found MC&D, and Bass ended up founding one of the precursor groups to the GOC… but what about Smith? The only thing I could think of based on his desire to report everything back to President Grant is that said president had him found the precursor to the FBI's UIU.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blah! Why did you change your username!? It makes reading old comment threads confusing~!

Anyway… Going back through everything because I think the site stopped tracking comments on some threads for some reason, and I want to make sure I'm not missing anything. I had apparently not upvoted this already, so +1 for you, good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's probably been stated before, but I'd like to toss in my 2 cents. I've read through all the 001 proposals, and in my mind this is the 'true' SCP-001. It's amazingly written, the unspeakable horrors and 'breeding pits' actually disturbed me (which is not easily done), and the fact that the 'researcher' actually gets an explanation rather than a bullet to the head just screams to me that they dug too deep and the Foundation wanted to give them the truth rather than leave a trail of breadcrumbs another researcher may pick up on. Not to mention that it's stated The Gate Guardian SCP is being used as a cover-up is the last detail that solidifies this as the true origin to me.
In short: Great article, I loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *bows.*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh mah GAWD! So, The Foundation came from the factory?! Holy shit when I read this I was in awe of what happened! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story says "The only one with an opinion was the old man". Shouldn't that be "the only one WITHOUT an opinion"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's just not my thing. Might be the tone of the voice. Great effort, though. Might not even be the tone…..there's just something I don't like, feels like the voice is trying to force some kind of drama out of the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lthNLg7JlAg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Technically, there's no overarching "real" SCP-001. I don't see a reason to squish this and Clef's proposal into the same article because both proposals inherently present a different thing as SCP-001.

Might make for an interesting tale, but I don't see the benefit of doing that to the proposals themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I may have a proposal to mix Clef's proposal with this. SCP-231 has a hidden poem in it that you can only observe if you copy paste the article. I suggest we do something similar to that here. The Angel was intended to be a distraction from the real SCP-001 in the story, so why not make it the same in the article? Using the same method to hide this entire message within Dr. Clef's proposal seems like it would be interesting. Though maybe Bright of Clef would rather only have one story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not my headcanon SCP-001 but the story is fantastic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, good execution. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, did I seriously not +1 till now? What is wrong with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although when we do end the SCP series I believe that this should be SCP-001 hands down as it was slightly better than the others in my opinion.

Again, you're totally free to believe that. The site's just never going to actually pick an SCP-001, though, even if the SCP series ends. Heck, people can still write SCP-001 proposals nowadays if they want, it's just a lot of work and effort. The fun of the 001 proposals is exploring the many possibilities and opening up different views of the Foundation universe. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I completely agree, we shouldn't designate an SCP-001 "The origin of all SCPs" until the site is done for good. Although when we do end the SCP series I believe that this should be SCP-001 hands down as it was slightly better than the others in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sort of. People are free to believe whatever they want to be true about the Foundation universe—so if they want to believe there is a "true" SCP-001, the site won't disprove that. However, naturally, the site will not confirm that either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With all this talk about possible Foundation/SCP origin stories and the like, there's one thing that I think some people miss:

We will never know the TRUE origin of everything, because it would only detract from the tone that this site attempts (successfully, might I add) to exude. As said countless times before, the true horror comes from the unknown, from that something that we can't see or feel but know is lurking just out of our weak grasps at understanding reality. All of this discussion is great, producing mind-bending arguments and theories, but the truth is, we should never have a named SCP-001. That would be like saying "Alright, folks, show's over. The monster some of you thought was a three-headed snake from the depths of Tartarus was really a Satanic two-headed werewolf from Mars who loves to stalk people with library cards." I love the idea of this site dangling shadowy truths above peoples' heads, and when we finally construct a shoddy ladder high enough to reach them, they're whisked ever higher, controlled by our own longing for mystery, imagination, and the hope for more.

So please, let the show go ever on…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really, scary? Hmm. Okay, we can go with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be correct.
